desk.rationale: Signal+Risk aligned bullish; Research neutral keeps size at half.
desk.plan: Enter FRSH at 10.20 with a stop at 10.00 (below support at 10.12) and a first target of 10.60 — that's a clean 2:1 R:R. Thesis: fresh momentum name with 14% 5d run, RSI not yet extended, and a healthy risk-on tape. The main risk is that Research is right — no fundamental catalyst means this unwinds fast if the market wobbles. Watch 10.12 support closely; any close below it is your early exit signal.

signal.note: Clean 5d momentum 14.16%, RSI 63.8 not overbought, bullish MACD, above VWAP. Fresh entry.

research.note: Clean technicals, strong 5d momentum +14%, but no catalyst. Pure momentum play.

risk.concern: Clean risk_on setup. RSI 63.8 not extreme, 14% 5d move suggests healthy momentum, not late entry.

desk.rationale: Risk veto stands: RSI 74.4 overbought + 6% 1d surge = crowded late chase.
desk.plan: Skip TROW. RSI at 74.4 after a 6% single-day surge is a textbook late-chase setup, and Risk has vetoed it. Signal agrees it's exhausted. No edge chasing this here.

signal.note: RSI 74.4 overbought, extended 5d run, 1d surge suggests exhaustion. Late chase.

research.note: MACD bullish cross, above VWAP, but soft 5d +6% and no fresh catalyst to sustain.

risk.concern: RSI 74.4 is overbought/extreme. Combined with 6% 1d pop, this is a crowded late chase.

desk.rationale: Research+Risk aligned; Signal neutral on mixed MACD holds size to half.
desk.plan: Enter CMCSA at 24.19, stop at 22.42 (just below support at 22.62), target 27.73 for a 2:1 R:R. Thesis: catalyst-driven move (+4.5% today, 3x volume on a fresh headline) with RSI at a neutral 51.9 — plenty of room to run. The risk: Signal flagged below-VWAP and no MACD confirmation, so if it can't reclaim VWAP quickly after entry, trim early. Wide stop means position size accordingly.

risk.concern: Solid risk_on regime. RSI 51.9 neutral, modest moves (4.5% 1d, 8.4% 5d), no extreme reading.

desk.rationale: Signal against + Research neutral + weak 5d momentum; no edge, noisy setup.
desk.plan: Pass on TTWO. Signal is actively against it — only 3% 5d momentum with RSI already at 67, this reads as choppy noise rather than real trend. No catalyst, no conviction from Research. Not worth the $13+ stop risk.

research.note: Decent technicals but only +3% 5d momentum. No catalyst, likely noise-driven bounce.

desk.plan: Hard pass on TER. All three advisors are against it and Risk has vetoed. A 6.3% single-day spike with only 1.3% 5d momentum is the classic gap-fill trap — you'd be the last buyer before it reverts. Stay away.

research.note: Spike +6.3% today but weak +1% 5d contradicts strength. Single-day spike on light volume.

risk.concern: 6.3% 1d spike but only 1.3% 5d. Classic one-day spike with no follow-through. Late chaser setup.

desk.rationale: Risk approves, both signal+research neutral/low conviction; regime ok but momentum fading.
desk.plan: Enter KHC at 24.18 with a stop at 22.25 (below support at 22.45) and target 28.05 — 2:1 R:R. Thesis: strong 5d momentum (+10%) with bullish MACD cross in a risk-on tape. Take only quarter size because both Signal and Research lack conviction — volume is thin and the 1d move is just 2%, suggesting the initial surge may already be fading. The thing that makes you wrong: momentum rolls over before it fills the gap to target, in which case 22.45 support break is your warning.

signal.note: Low volume rel 1.13, modest 1d 1.98% despite bullish MACD and 5d strength. Lacks conviction.

research.note: Good 5d +10% and bullish MACD cross, but missing volume catalyst. Momentum fading.

risk.concern: Risk_on approved, RSI 60.6 reasonable. 5d move 9.7% is solid, but 1d only 2%, may be extended already.

synthesis.summary: DELL is up 5% today but the only supplied news item — a Robinhood product launch — has no connection to Dell's business. The move is unexplained by available news; likely driven by sector rotation, options flow, or a catalyst not captured in the provided headlines.
